drink.name: Café con leche
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review as superb and perfectly served; other drink mentions are generic.
drink.quote: The café con leche was superb and the perfect temperature

food.name: Ceviche
food.reason: Mentioned across multiple reviews and called 'a must' in a top review, though one reviewer found it slightly underwhelming.
food.quote: the incredible ceviche (a must)

reviewSample.text: This restaurant is incredible—run, don’t walk to grab a spot. They are open for breakfast - dinner, so you have options!  The food is a mix of traditional South American offerings, but somehow light and refreshingly bright and zingy, not heavy.  Incredible flavors blended so well to complement each other—The pickled onions, perfectly ripened avocados, thin plantain chips (not thick or greasy), unique avocado sauce, beans cooked perfectly (not mushy)…everything was just beautifully matched.  We had Arupa vegetable, spinach empanadas, the incredible ceviche (a must) and the yucca fries (lightly friend, but again not greasy).  The atmosphere is modern chic, yet cozy, intimate and friendly.  Seems to seat 40-50 including a bar area.  Plenty of incredible drinks of course.  Great spot.  You feel the careful considered choices in the menu and surroundings.  They have interesting events to try too—Thursday night Spanish guitar, Sunday cozy mornings with live music among them.  We tried the unique xocolatl chocolate too which was super.  Thank you for a great meal!

I stopped by for a solo lunch, and the experience was fantastic. The service was incredibly quick and efficient, which I really appreciated.
The portions are on the smaller side, but they are flavorful and well-presented. A standout moment was when the owner personally checked in on me to ensure everything was perfect; it’s rare to see that level of care, and it made the visit feel very welcoming.
If I lived in this neighborhood, this would easily be my go-to spot. Whether you're visiting the area or live nearby, I highly recommend stopping in!

Stellar experience at El Viñedo Local. Great atmosphere and fantastic coffee. The café con leche was superb and the perfect temperature to drink right away. Also ordered a guava pastelito and it was fantastic. Our server Malcolm was so wonderful, very attentive, kind, and welcoming. A must try for anyone in the midtown area looking for a great coffee and yummy bite in a relaxed, well-designed atmosphere with a ton of natural light!

drink.name: Bottomless Mimosas
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers praise the mimosas, with one noting they're freshly squeezed and continuously refilled.
drink.quote: Bottomless mimosas were freshly squeezed and always coming

food.name: Mini Muffins
food.reason: The complimentary mini muffins are mentioned by nearly every reviewer as a delightful signature touch, like a charming bread-and-butter equivalent for brunch.
food.quote: Offered delicious mini muffins which is so cute for a breakfast spot

reviewSample.text:
Freddy was an awesome server. He’s energy was great and he was super helpful. We got the French toast and a side of corn beef hash. Both were delicious and worth the cost.
The cold brew was delicious especially with the complimentary mini muffins.

Don’t skip the attached bakery. The pretzel croissant and cheese pocket were amazing. We also loved the chocolate croissant and the almond croissant. Honestly, you can’t go wrong. Their food is so delicious and memorable.

We’ll definitely be back.

Glad to have finally tried this lovely café after a friend from book club recommended it! I've driven past it several times and had been missing out on this gem. Last week I made it for a late brunch and enjoyed the mini muffins  appetizer and highlight was the delicious chicken and waffles! And server Anthony(io) was great, the chicken was sprinkled with cheddar cheese a yummy bonus and the chillii syrup added nice kick! My bellini was good too, such a nice space and got to meet the manager & chat with her & she was lovely as well. Can't wait to come back!

First and foremost: make a reservation! We came on an overcast Sunday that was supposedly “less busy than usual” but we definitely would’ve had a wait if not for the booking. Offered delicious mini muffins which is so cute for a breakfast spot, their version of bread and olive oil. Second of all, if they have the French toast Monte Cristo (monthly special but hopefully comes back), that should be your meal. If you’re a fan of sweet and savory and can’t have your pancakes without some eggs, this is THE MEAL! Not even a fan of ham of mustard but this sandwich transcended preferences, especially dipped in some maple syrup. Bottomless mimosas were freshly squeezed and always coming. The shrimp omelette was delicious and not as potent as you would think from the ingredient description. The grits were not great, would recommend the potatoes over them. Seating was quick, wait staff was attentive and friendly, food was out in 10 minutes, overall a wonderful experience and would be back again!

drink.name: Hot chocolate
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers specifically praised the hot chocolate in various forms (truffle, Mexican, regular), calling it exquisite and excellent.
drink.quote: The truffle hot chocolate was excellent as well — rich, smooth

food.name: Cheesecake
food.reason: Desserts, especially cheesecakes, are repeatedly highlighted as a standout, with one reviewer specifically raving about the cookies and cream cheesecake.
food.quote: huge selection of cheesecakes and tortes is often mentioned as a must-try

reviewSample.text:
Café Intermezzo is a charming European-style café and restaurant right in the heart of Midtown Atlanta — great for coffee, desserts, brunch, lunch, or late-night treats. The atmosphere feels cozy, upscale, and a bit romantic, with rich wood tones, soft lighting, and elegant décor that make it a nice place to relax or catch up with friends.  ￼

Highlights:
☕ Excellent coffee, cappuccinos, and specialty drinks — perfect any time of day.  ￼
🍰 Desserts and cakes are a standout — the huge selection of cheesecakes and tortes is often mentioned as a must-try.  ￼
🍽️ The menu covers breakfast, brunch, lunch, and dinner with options like crepes, salmon pasta, salads, and French classics.  ￼
🌙 It’s also open late night on weekends, which makes it a fun spot for a post-dinner dessert or drink.  ￼

What to Know:
• Seating is first-come, first-served, and it can get busy during peak brunch and weekend dinner hours.  ￼
• Paid parking nearby is convenient and often validated by the café.  ￼
• Some dishes get mixed reviews — desserts and drinks usually shine more than some savory entrees.  ￼

Overall: A go-to Midtown spot for great coffee, decadent desserts, and European café vibes — ideal for brunch dates, dessert outings, or a relaxed nightcap.

This place is top notch and very aesthetic. Lots of coffee and dessert options. The tiramisu and hot chocolate were exquisite. The hostess was very warm and polite. There was a wait time about 15 mins on weekends.  Note - there is no free parking nearby.

Café Intermezzo — Midtown exceeded my expectations. I enjoyed the best brochette I’ve ever had: the bread was fresh and pleasantly crisp, the tomatoes and sauce were absolutely outstanding, and every bite was full of flavor. The truffle hot chocolate was excellent as well — rich, smooth, and a perfect finish. Highly recommended.

drink.name: Cash Money Matcha
drink.reason: Mentioned by name in two 5-star reviews with enthusiastic praise; one reviewer ordered two rounds, another highlighted the cinnamon & vanilla twist.
drink.quote: The cinnamon & vanilla twist really set it off!!!

food.name: Red velvet muffin
food.reason: Only specifically named food item, described as one of the best baked goods the reviewer has had.
food.quote: easily one of the best baked goods I've had

reviewSample.text: I came here recently for the first time to meet a friend. He had also not been here even though he doesn't live too far away. We were both so impressed with the space and the professional and friendly staff. We met one of the owners and she was credibly generous with her time and sharing about how the business started. Entrepreneurship community and empowerment is a huge theme throughout the coffee shop.  There was a constant stream of customers coming in and out getting coffee and a few of us were sitting and having our breakfast or pastry with our coffee and drinks. The bathroom was clean and the common spaces were kept nice and tidy. There are also spaces to sit outside but not too many. The coffee was delicious and I bought one of their branded coffee beans for home. My wife and I loved brewing it and it tasted delicious. The one we had was from Guatemala.

I knew they would be good, but I didn’t know they would be great. I ordered the Cash Money Matcha and had to get two rounds—one for me and one for my sister. The drinks were amazing, but what really stood out was the atmosphere and music selection, which were absolutely top tier.

This place is a true example of Black excellence, while also showcasing business excellence overall. It attracts a diverse crowd, and I love how intentional and cultured everything feels—from the product names to the artwork throughout the space. On top of that, the customer service was A1.

If you’re looking for a go-to coffee or tea spot in Atlanta, be sure to give The Black Coffee Atlanta a try. I highly recommend it and will definitely be back again.

Before heading to church, I decided to explore some local coffee shops and stumbled upon Black Coffee. I was instantly drawn in by its earthy atmosphere, which provided a perfect blend of comfort and inspiration; ideal for working on my laptop or simply unwinding. One feature that particularly caught my eye was a beautifully curated wall celebrating black history, which added a depth to the space.

The friendly staff at the counter greeted us warmly and inquired about our morning, instantly making us feel appreciated. As for the menu, it boasted some intriguing options, including a latte infused with cinnamon and a rich brown sugar syrup—a delightful twist that piqued my interest.

While the prices leaned towards the higher end, the exceptional quality of both the coffee and the service made every cent worthwhile. I left feeling satisfied and inspired, and I will definitely be returning for another visit!

drink.name: Lychee Refresher
drink.reason: Enthusiastically praised in a 5-star review; other drink mentions (cappuccino, generic coffee) were merely 'okay' or unspecified.
drink.quote: the lychee refresher was so good I hope they keep it on the menu

food.name: Matcha Bun
food.reason: Specifically named and praised in a 4-star review as soft, freshly baked with generous filling; buns are a recurring highlight across reviews.
food.quote: Matcha bun and Violet I enjoyed so much... soft, and tasted freshly baked

reviewSample.text:
While exploring the area, I was enticed to give this bakery & cafe shop a visit for an afternoon snack.

I've ordered
Violet $3.95
Matcha Bun $2.95
Cappucino $4.95
Tiramisu Cup - TO GO $6.95
Sales Tax:
Overall Total with Tips $23.54

The Matcha bun and Violet (taro filling) I enjoyed so much, very familiar asian style of pastries. Dense bread with generous filling, soft, and tasted freshly baked. Capuccino was okay, and the Tiramisu that my husband enjoyed. Very friendly and attentive staff.

Happy tummy and happy customer.

Such a cutesy and quaint bakery/cafe. I’ve been to a few that are styled similar, where there are displays cases upon walking in with shelves of individually wrapped goodies.

You can grab a tray and choose what you want from the cases. There’s all kinds of sweet and savory breads, mochi and other Asian-inspired sweets. And then at the back you can order coffee or cold desserts like cake, tarts or cheesecake.

Everything was so delicious and felt like they really put thought and love into the delicacies. I got a coffee and it was sweet but was the perfect warm drink on a chilly night. It’s so cute and plenty of families sat down to enjoy the sweets and hang out.

Sweet Hut is one of my favorite spots in Atlanta! The pastries are always fresh, light, and full of flavor — you can tell they take pride in what they make. The place is super clean, the vibe is great, and the customer service is always friendly and welcoming. It’s also a nice spot to chill, study, or get some work done with a coffee or tea.

Only downside — parking on Peachtree Street can be a headache, and you’ll probably have to pay for it. But honestly, once you’re inside, it’s totally worth it.

Highly recommend if you’re looking for good pastries, great service, and a comfortable place to unwind!

drink.name: Fountain of Youth
drink.reason: Specifically named green drink praised as refreshing and great post-workout. Other reviews mention smoothies generally but only this one is named.
drink.quote: a green drink called the fountain of youth that was super yummy and refreshing

food.name: Pregame Bowl
food.reason: Specifically named bowl with positive context as a post-workout recovery meal. Bowls are mentioned positively across multiple reviews, but this is the only one named.
food.quote: I ordered the pregame bowl... The perfecttttttt way to recover after a workout

reviewSample.text:
I have frequented E+Rose a few times before but I had to acknowledge Associate/Manager John for his kindness yesterday.

I ordered through Uber Eats yesterday and didn’t realize I ordered at the wrong location. John was very understanding and accommodating with getting my order right and making sure that I still felt valued even though it was a mistake on my end.

Thank you John for being understanding, caring, and accommodating with everything! You truly made my morning with letting me feel special despite my mistake and being so nice with the service you gave. You are AMAZING! 🙏🏾💜

I stopped by E+Rose in Atlanta and had such a great experience! I tried one of the bowls and a worker whose name was stated as Makayla was incredibly helpful in assisting me. Her service was fast, friendly, and truly went above and beyond. The environment was very welcoming and had a great vibe overall. I also want to mention a fellow named John! He was very friendly and helpful as well. I’d definitely recommend anyone to stop by this location. You’ll be taken care of!

Amazing experience at E+Rose. Our barista John was so nice and welcoming. The atmosphere was very nice and the selection of smoothies are amazing! Will definitely be returning and I encourage everyone to go especially if you live in the west midtown area and you want healthier options!

drink.reason: No specific drinks are named in the reviews; only general references to a café exist.

food.name: Fish tacos
food.reason: Specifically praised as fresh and flavorful with a 9.2/10 rating in a detailed review. Other dishes received mixed reviews, while fish tacos earned the highest specific score.
food.quote: Fish taco: Fresh and flavorful

reviewSample.text:
This place truly feels like stepping into a vibrant Mexican mercado. The colors, music, aromas, and energy create such a fun and authentic atmosphere. Every corner is full of life and culture.
The food is fresh, flavorful, and beautifully prepared — you can taste the quality and tradition in every bite. The service was friendly, warm, and made us feel right at home.
It’s not just a meal, it’s an experience. Perfect for families, friends, or anyone looking to enjoy great food in a lively, unique setting. This new spot is a must-visit and definitely deserves all the support!

Loved this place SO MUCH! I came to Atlanta for a weekend for the event and wanted to explore some options in the city and saw this place on Maps. The place is GORGEOUS! The prices are super affordable for the service and quality they provide. And the Mexican shop that is connected to the cafe makes the place even better. If you want to dive into true Mexican culture - just go there!

I stopped in during a four‑hour layover and this spot was a great find. The café and the cantina next door are literally connected and share a patio, but the café has a quieter vibe with classic coffee‑shop offerings and a small school‑store section featuring Latino handmade merchandise.

I ordered the lunch special with a chile relleno and fish tacos. The food sits right between authentic Mexican and Tex‑Mex in both flavor and presentation.

- Fish taco: Fresh and flavorful — especially after tweaking the garnishes. 9.2/10
- Chile relleno: The batter was a bit firm and the four‑cheese blend was a little heavy, but still tasty. 7/10
- Chips & salsa: Excellent. Ask for the habanero crisps on the side — that takes it to an 11/10
- Red velvet cheesecake: Rich, decadent, and worth trying. 8.5/10

Huge shoutout to Charlie, who was running the café solo and still delivered friendly, attentive service. Great spot for a layover break.

drink.name: Chai latte
drink.reason: Enthusiastically praised in a 5-star review for perfect balance of cinnamon and sugar; other drink mentions were lukewarm.
drink.quote: The best chai that I have had in a while!

food.name: Lox Plate
food.reason: Specifically raved about in a 5-star review with detailed praise for freshness, toasted everything bagel, and bright capers and cucumber.
food.quote: The Lox Plate and Oh My it was SO good!

reviewSample.text: The best chai that I have had in a while! I had it hot with soy milk and it was DELICIOUS! With just the right balance of cinnamon and sugar. The Lox Plate and Oh My it was SO good! Everything was fresh, the everything bagel perfectly toasted and the capers and cucumber were something that I haven't had on it in other locations before and it added a nice brightness and crunch to the dish. My companions loved their waffles and pancakes and specialty drinks. The atmosphere was great with ample seating, great service, friendly staff and a cool location. Just be aware that the narrow street that it's located on is actually a 2 way road and can be tricky to navigate by car when all the parking covers the one side. Cute area too with great artwork in the restaurant. Highly recommend!

I haven’t been there for a long time. I remember the last time that I went had a delicious omelette and it was a little place. It has definitely grown in size and the menu is kind of weird to understand now ..I got a pizza which I didn’t know was gonna be on a tortilla shell, but it was OK but then when we order the empanadas, I’m not sure why there was peas and carrots in it or Why?. It was served with Mexican salsa. They just don’t go together super weird but the vibe was very nice.

What a cool place!! Loved the vibes of this place. Food was delicious and the staff was friendly. We got the gyro and the steak sandwich. It comes with fries and a salad. Definitely will return in the future and try out new items.

drink.name: Iced Latte
drink.reason: Lattes receive the most specific praise across reviews, including 'best latte I had in Atlanta' for the seasonal brown butter rosemary latte and explicit love for the iced latte. Espresso straight was criticized as sour and bitter.
drink.quote: The iced latte and mocha were so amazing!

food.reason: No specific food item is named positively. One reviewer notes they are 'always out of pastry,' and no other food is mentioned.

reviewSample.text: The iced latte and mocha were so amazing! Dancing Goats had a great atmosphere. Our barista was so helpful. The swinging daybeds were so cozy and comfortable. Indoor and outdoor seating is available. A must visit in Atlanta!

Stopped by this morning to grab an espresso for the first time. I really like this location and especially love the outdoor spaces to sit and hang out. This is a huge plus. The espresso was sour and bitter and was not the best I’ve had however. I’m sure if you’re enjoying a sweetened latte or iced drink you may not be able to notice but I could immediately tell the shots were not pulling properly when I tasted the espresso straight.

Random note…The bathrooms here were super nice. Probably the nicest coffee shop bathrooms I’ve seen lol. 😆 I just wanted to note I was impressed.

They also sell a lot of cool specialty coffee stuff like V60s, filters etc…

I love dancing goats coffee! I’ve been to all locations in Georgia, i love the atmosphere here the most but they are always out of pastry. My go to spot is the one in midtown right next to the midtown marta station, they are awesome too!

drink.name: Nitro Iced Coffee
drink.reason: Specific repeat-customer praise in a positive review; other drinks each mentioned only once.
drink.quote: nitro iced coffee. I really really enjoy it

food.name: Gruyère Twist
food.reason: Mentioned positively in two separate reviews, including a 5-star review calling it 'excellent'.
food.quote: the Gruyère twist was excellent!

reviewSample.text:
Atmosphere: The shop was nice and open, but I went right before the remodel, so the walls were bare. Can't wait to see how they decorate.
Drinks: I got the s'mores latte, which was very good. I thought it would be a little sweeter, so I had to add sugar, but I have a very sweet tooth, so it's probably fine for most people. I also got a chai latte with vanilla, and that was great. No added sugar needed :)
Food: I got the Gruyere twist, which was delicious. It made for a perfect little breakfast.
Great place for espresso. Lovely inside and out. Very nice pull, but thought the taste was a bit bland. Think they rotate beans, so maybe another day would have more interesting flavor.

My Saturday routine includes a stop at Taproom for their nitro iced coffee. I really really enjoy it. The service is always fast, coffee has a nice smooth taste. Parking can be tricky if you get here during peak hours but you will find a spot. There’s a public lot next to it which gets pretty packed around 10am but you should still be able to find street parking. I always get the coffee, they have pastries which I’ve gotten in the past. They’ve been pretty good but I come here for the coffee!! Employees are super nice and friendly, lots of people come through with their kids!

drink.name: Matcha
drink.reason: The only specifically named drink with positive praise, called 'perfect' in a 5-star review at a fair price.
drink.quote: Came to try their matcha, it was perfect!

food.name: Pumpkin scone
food.reason: The only food item named in any review, described as delicious in a 5-star review.
food.quote: tried the pumpkin scone and it was delicious

reviewSample.text:
Ordered 4 shots of espresso around 3:30pm to make a cocktail in the evening. Staff called out a double shot, but other staff member corrects her and says it's four shots. I walk up and it is obviously only a double shot. I asked her to confirm that is four shots and both staff members say "yes, it's four." I needed it in a larger cup that I could put a lid on, and when I poured the espresso in the 8oz cup, it filled up the bottom inch or so. I asked AGAIN to confirm this is 4 shots instead of a double, and they say no again. A small taste showed these were clearly not ristretto shots, which would justify the lack of volume in the shot.

I'm now faced with a conundrum: do I accept this incorrect assertion, and go about my day not receiving what I paid for, or do I become the kind of person that argues with the already somewhat rude staff after being told objectively incorrect information twice. I chose the former, but the experience did not leave me. In order to prove to myself that I am not crazy, I got a double shot at a different coffee shop, which is pictured in this review on the left. Despite their efforts at gaslighting, I know what a double shot looks like, as any person who has worked in a coffee shop will.

What could have been a simple miscommunication became a very odd situation in which the staff dug their heels in. Obviously, I will not be coming back.

Came to try their matcha, it was perfect! Very good matcha at a fair price :) they had a very nice sélection of baked goods, i tried the pumpkin scone and it was delicious. I will definetly come back :) VERY FRIENDLY STAFF

Got a large Americano and was told after they gave it to me that they short changed me a shot of espresso as I began walking out. If the barista is in training that is fine but it is a simple drink and they handed it to me after the cashier told her she didn’t put enough in it. Taking my drink back after you handed it to me is also a health code violation. Not the first time I have been skimped at this location but will be the last. Go to dancing goats on North Ave if you want this coffee.

drink.name: Cold Press
drink.reason: Specifically mentioned and customized to the reviewer's liking in a 5-star review; other reviews note good strong coffee generally.
drink.quote: fixed my cold press fit to my liking

food.name: Turkey Sandwich on Asiago
food.reason: Directly praised as 'amazing' in a 5-star review; the only specific food item named.
food.quote: The turkey sandwich on Asiago is amazing

reviewSample.text: The service has greatly improved and I'm excited to make this a ritual from time to time. Thank you Katie and team for calming my morning and remembering my order after only one visit! I don't expect this but it was absolutely amazing and helpful:)

Currently writing this in the parking lot lol. Katie was super sweet, it was my first time trying Caribou and she helped me become a member and even fixed my cold press fit to my liking🥹. Gave some great recommendations as well, I’ll definitely be back. The turkey sandwich on Asiago is amazing !

It’s really no wonder Caribou failed to launch. Could’ve been a starby’s competitor. Employees here don’t open up on time and 8/9 employees act like it’s a favor to serve you. Even with the Starbucks closed around the corner no one goes here. Why would they? Only due to location have they not failed. This was the singular choice for a coffee shop for 5 days as I was staying at hotel within walking distance so experiences are over several days.

drink.name: Iced chai latte
drink.reason: Praised in multiple reviews, including the dirty iced chai called 'amazing' and another described as nicely spiced and not overly sweet.
drink.quote: I got a dirty iced chai it was amazing!!

food.name: Toasted everything bagel with cream cheese
food.reason: Only specifically named food item in the reviews and described positively.
food.quote: Simple, but really good!

reviewSample.text:
Foxtail Coffee in Midtown Atlanta is one of those easy go-to spots that just works. I ordered a café latte with oat milk, and it was smooth, well-balanced, and comforting without being heavy. My friend got the iced chai latte, and it was equally good—nicely spiced and not overly sweet.

The space feels especially cozy right now with their Christmassy touches, including holiday merchandise that adds a festive vibe without being over the top. I also appreciated that they offer some gluten-free snack options, which is a thoughtful bonus.

Overall, Foxtail is a reliable spot for studying or getting work done—good drinks, a comfortable atmosphere, and a calm Midtown energy that makes it easy to settle in for a while.

The vibe here is super cool! Service was also very fast although they were very direct, could see some people saying they are rude but I didn’t get that. I got a dirty iced chai it was amazing!! Not pictured was a toasted everything bagel with cream cheese. Simple, but really good!

Pretty bummed. I went here for the first time since moving to midtown and was SHOCKED at how expensive it is. I ordered a “regular”, and after a 10% tip it was over $8. I was handed my coffee and was shocked at how light it felt. After walking home and taking the lid off to reheat it, I saw that the cup wasn’t even halfway full, and I hadn’t taken a single sip yet. I understand whipped cream takes up space, but it shouldn’t take up half the cup.

drink.name: Chai Latte
drink.reason: Received an enthusiastic 10/10 in a 5-star review for being not too sweet with great cinnamon. Other drinks (macchiato, Brown Bear Coffee, mocha, latte) also praised but each only once.
drink.quote: best chai latte i've ever had. not too sweet

food.name: Vegan walnut banana bread
food.reason: Only food item specifically named in the reviews, described as delicious.
food.quote: vegan walnut banana bread which was delicious

reviewSample.text:
Aurora Coffee was recommended to me by a local to Atlanta. I had ordered the cortado with soy milk without realizing the cortado proportions were different than a standard cortado. I was going to pay for their macchiato (which is half espresso half milk) but the staff gave it to me on the house since I didn’t know the proportions when I ordered the cortado. It was really kind and I would have paid for it.

The macchiato was exactly what I was looking for. More of that espresso flavor coming through balanced with the milk.

For food, I got a vegan walnut banana bread which was delicious.

I also bought a bag of bean from Guatemala to enjoy at home. The staff was friendly with explaining the different flavors and tasting notes of the different coffee beans they had which was super helpful.

The vibe and ambience inside is quite cute. I found it cozy and a little alternative - a perfect mix. There’s space inside and outside to sit and enjoy coffee and snacks. I also saw several people working from their laptops.

I’d definitely recommend Aurora Coffee! Just be sure to double check the proportions if that’s something important to you ☺️

Visited multiple times w friends: great place to spend some time at while browsing little five points.
Great atmosphere and very friendly workers!!

I had an amazing time stumbling upon Aurora Coffee. I hopped off the bus to go vintage shopping when I saw this graffiti filled piece of art on the corner which was Aurora. I walked in pleasantly greeted by the staff, I had obvious questions that could be answered by reading the menu but the girl at the counter was helpful and kind. I got the Brown Bear Coffee with an extra shop of espresso, I was able to add sweeteners myself with the sugar counter, and stepped outside to enjoy the sun. Would absolutely return and for $6.50 I was very happy.

Thanks again!

drink.name: House blend drip coffee
drink.reason: Only specific coffee named; described as standard with chocolatey notes, served at good temperature. General praise for coffee quality in other reviews.
drink.quote: hint of sweetness and chocolatey notes

food.name: Sausage, egg, and cheese biscuit
food.reason: Named specifically in a 5-star review with 'yummy' praise. The other named item (Kirkwood Sandwich) received a 2-star review for lacking flavor.
food.quote: Yummy breakfast sandwiches... sausage, egg, and cheese biscuit

reviewSample.text:
I love this place!!! Yummy breakfast sandwiches and coffee with a warm community feel. I got the sausage, egg, and cheese biscuit. There are events and posters with games to play. Staff was very rude but maybe they had a bad day. Pretty loud here, if you want to work or call I'd time it properly. I would return to check out the outdoor space.

edit: just read through some of the reviews, seems like the rudeness is not a one-day event haha. being friendly is free!

I love this place. Living in Inman park, you’ve gotta go back into the surrounding neighborhoods to find anything eclectic and vibey. Awesome, women-led staff who know how to brew some dang good coffee and cook up dang good food. Dog-friendly, which I absolutely love. They’ve made a regular outta me.

I drove from Decatur this morning to try Park Grounds because they offer vegetarian options. I ordered the Kirkwood Sandwich with veggie sausage on a croissant, and the croissant itself was absolutely perfect. However, the fried egg could have used some basic seasoning like salt and pepper, and I would have preferred a more flavorful cheese choice. While the sandwich was well made, it ultimately lacked flavor.

As someone who’s been vegetarian for 14 years, I know many of us are willing to go out of our way—especially for breakfast—to find good vegetarian food in the city, but it has to deliver on taste. No knock on Park Grounds, but unless the food is better seasoned, I don’t see myself returning.

drink.name: Oat milk latte
drink.reason: Multiple mentions; described as solid and good value, though not exceptional.
drink.quote: large oat milk latte which was fine

food.name: Ham and Gouda flatbread breakfast sandwich
food.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review; sandwiches generally called delicious and yummy across reviews.
food.quote: the Ham and Gouda on flat bread is so good

reviewSample.text:
Great coffee shop! Awesome service, decent amount of seating inside and outside, solid coffee, and yummy food!

Totally unexpected, but staff brought coffee and food to me (and others!) when ready—not necessary, but appreciated and truly great service.

Wasn’t expecting too much from my turkey pesto sandwich, but it was so yummy and perfectly crispy while not being oily.

My iced latte wasn’t anything crazy good but solid and veryyy good for the price (only $4.70 for a large!)

I was able to find free street parking right next to the shop, but I can suspect it to be hard during busy times on weekends.

Drip is a nice coffee shop in a quiet neighborhood. There is plenty of inside and outside seating.
The wait staff did acknowledge me saying he would be right with me and he wore a mask. I ordered my oar milk latte and had a seat in the comfortable chairs.
I got a large oat milk latte which was fine. The coffee could have been a little richer.
They have an all genders restroom which was neat and clean
There is plenty of street parking  but I went on a wee day. Not sure how much parking there would be on the weekend or later in the afternoon

I love all things coffee and tea. I like parusing these shops all over the world especially in my own backyard. I've only had good experiences here. Patrons: We dont always have good days, if a server is rude maybe offer some kind words because you never know what someone is going through. Have some compassion.

drink.name: Hot chai latte with oat milk
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review as warm, creamy, and delicious. Other reviews only mention coffee generically.
drink.quote: warm, creamy, and absolutely delicious

food.name: Pesto chicken sandwich on ciabatta
food.reason: Detailed praise in a 5-star review highlighting the crisp ciabatta, perfectly seasoned chicken, and fresh pesto.
food.quote: chicken was seasoned to perfection, and the pesto was so fresh

reviewSample.text:
Surrounded by the loud and busy of the seemingly essential, Urban Grind is a humble piece of strong and sweet nestled in its own buoyant cloud of artistic peace. Obsidian to the noise, It nourishes the potential to neutralize the chaos, and transform any heart and soul that crosses its threshold. I read (prior to my visit) that this place has been considered- for quite some time - to be an institution that holds sanctuary for the creator.
Any establishment that supports the arts is a gem in my book.
Go and vibe, Tribe.

I had the most wonderful experience at Urban Grind on Marietta Street in Atlanta! I stopped by today and tried their hot chai latte with oat milk—it was warm, creamy, and absolutely delicious. I also had a pesto chicken sandwich on perfectly crisp ciabatta bread—the chicken was seasoned to perfection, and the pesto was so fresh. For dessert, I had a vegan zucchini walnut loaf, and I couldn’t believe it was vegan—it was so flavorful and moist. The customer service was excellent, truly. They went above and beyond to make sure every need was met, and the atmosphere is so quaint and quiet—perfect for a relaxing visit. I can’t wait to come back—highly recommend this gem!

Urban Grind is a cozy retreat offering tasty coffee and satisfying, hot foods. The interior’s small space is furnished with comfortable seating in different arrangements ensuring you’ll have a spot that matches the mood whether you are looking to connect with coworkers or silently read in peace. The staff is very helpful and friendly, and will add to your pleasant experience.

drink.name: Latte
drink.reason: The latte is praised for rich espresso and microfoam in a detailed 4-star review; matcha latte also highlighted but latte has more substantive description.
drink.quote: lots of microfoam and was rich with espresso

food.name: Rosemary Ham Sandwich
food.reason: Specifically named with positive flavor notes; sandwiches are also generally praised in another review.
food.quote: buttery crispy bread...The flavor was definitely there

reviewSample.text: Vibe is a 10/10! I had the matcha latte with lavender syrup and it was delicious. Love that they also have a DJ on some nights, plus games, books, and art for sale. Such a fun and unique spot!

Condesa Coffee is a nice coffee shop. There is plenty of indoor and outdoor seating.
I got a latte with whole milk and hubby got drip coffee. The latte had lots of microfoam and was rich with espresso. They had ceramic cups, which I prefer. The drip coffee was whole body and rich.
There is plenty of street parking as well as a parking deck. They have two bathrooms.
Fantastic! Came for Slow Mornings Coworking with Maison Mogul Consulting and the service and menu are top tier! Choices of tea, coffee and the sandwiches! Yummy! Highly recommend! Lovely service!

drink.name: Iced coffee
drink.reason: Specifically named in a 5-star review with positive framing; other reviews praise coffee generally as smooth and flavorful.
drink.quote: Kind invitation to order a delicious iced coffee

food.name: Empanada
food.reason: Only specific food item named in reviews, described positively alongside the coffee.
food.quote: an empanada that was just as delicious

reviewSample.text: cute interiors but little seating indoors and is very much an in-and-out place. coffee is strong-ish with a solid selection. would def come back if in the area!

Super friendly and welcoming staff. First time checking this place out, and will certainly return for seconds!

Kind invitation to order a delicious iced coffee, on a beautiful Sunday afternoon. What I liked most about this location, was being tucked away in a quaint neighborhood. My favorite type of spot!

The place was just small enough to grab your cup of joe, and head out to the patio or front porch area to sip away. After hanging out on the porch for a little bit, and feeling like there was too much cat hair for one to bear, we decided to walk the neighborhood instead. Just what I needed.

This was my first time coming to con leche. I’m a coffee lover and let me tell you, their coffee was so good, what made this visit outstanding however, was the customer service. The barista was so friendly, we told him we were visiting from out of town and he shared some recommendations for food places. He was also very knowledgeable about the coffee. I will definitely go by con leche next time I’m in town!

drink.name: Cortado
drink.reason: Specifically praised in multiple 5-star reviews for perfectly pulled espresso shots and excellent quality.
drink.quote: The cortado had perfectly pulled shots of espresso

food.name: Chocolate croissant
food.reason: Mentioned in a 5-star review as part of amazing pastries; most specific food item named.
food.quote: The pastries were amazing. We had the chocolate croissant

reviewSample.text:
Good
Hot
Coffee
-I'm a little biased about East Pole, mainly because I've never had a bad experience. The staff is very kind and open to making suggestions and recommendations. There's usually more space at the Armour Yards location, but you definitely want to visit the Virginia Highlands location when in the area.

Amazing specialty coffee shop. I tried the espresso and they dialed it in perfectly. Lots of great flavors and no bitterness. The pastries were amazing. We had the chocolate croissant, blueberry muffin and another pastry with some jam on it. The space is wide open and very nice inside. You can also watch them roasting their coffee which is really fun. I highly recommend this spot if you love espresso, a good cortado or lattes!

Espresso was good. You can tell that the beans are good quality and the roasting is done well. How's the espresso had almost no crema and also  not very hot. My advice: increase the temperature and figure out how to get some crema. Great atmosphere.

drink.name: Flat White
drink.reason: Specifically praised in multiple reviews as rich, creamy, and lovely. Most-named specialty drink with positive descriptors.
drink.quote: extremely rich and creamy. An absolutely delicious drink

food.name: Breakfast Sandwich
food.reason: The only specific food item named, recommended in multiple reviews and tied to the butcher shop angle.
food.quote: Be sure to try their breakfast sandwiches

reviewSample.text: Cute coffee shop/butcher shop with tasty coffee and breakfast sandwiches! We went on a cold day so i noticed the lack of indoor seating but i think it would be a nice place to go when the weather is warmer as they have lots of benches and seating out front. The coffee came out very quickly and the breakfast sandwiches from the butcher came out a few minutes after. It was about $43 for two small specialty coffees and two sandwiches which is a bit steep (the drinks were $7 a piece).

Incredible coffee shop bringing incredible baristas, product, and recipes to the table. The most interesting menu of all time plus all the classics you’d want. Be sure to try their breakfast sandwiches along with a experimental coffee order!

A buddy of mine had mentioned this place to me and as we are both big into coffee I had to give it a try . I've got to say I wasn't disappointed, I ordered a flat white which was extremely rich and creamy. An absolutely delicious drink. I had also ordered I am Salsa and this drink, this drink went so well not only with evening that was going on in the drink but it complimented the flat white perfectly.

If I had not been given an address I might have overlooked it. I had walked passed it and had to do a double take to make sure I was at the right spot.

Definitely want to try their lunch another day.

Both drinks were amazing.
Atmosphere seemed pleasant but being that it doubles as a butcher shop it kinda threw me off . However definitely stop on by and give them a try. The goal will be to go through the summer menu as it looks like they do special drinks for a season or through rotation.

drink.name: Matcha
drink.reason: Only specifically named drink, praised as 'very yummy' in a 5-star review. Other reviews praise coffee generically.
drink.quote: Got a matcha which was very yummy

food.name: Almond croissant
food.reason: Explicitly highly recommended by a 5-star reviewer; the only named food item.
food.quote: HIGHLY recommend the almond croissant

reviewSample.text: Super cute coffee shop! Tucked away under the apartment complex but a nice spot. Got a matcha which was very yummy. The baked goods also looked really good. Great service and WiFi was strong. Comfy seating and a lot of space which I always appreciate. Parking is a little tight, but there’s a big lot in the back for a couple minute walk to the shop. They provide coupon for the parking inside once you register your car which is always appreciated. Will definitely be coming back!

What a cute coffee shop! Drinks were delicious and HIGHLY recommend the almond croissant.
The staff was very attentive and kind. Even though they were busy, they made sure to come check in on us and make sure we liked our drinks and had everything we needed.

My favorite coffee shop in Atlanta. I come here a lot to knock out work and the staff is sooo friendly and the vibe is great. Whoever is on the aux as well gets a 5 star as well! The coffee is always consistent no matter who’s working. I have no notes for suggestions/improvements.

drink.name: Latte
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers praised the latte, with one calling it 'one of the best lattes I've had.' The spiced apple latte was also highlighted in a high-star review.
drink.quote: One of the best lattes I've had

food.name: Croissant
food.reason: The chocolate croissant was specifically praised in two separate reviews as delicious, making it the most consistently recommended food item.
food.quote: muffin and croissant delicious

reviewSample.text: This little coffee shop is so cute! They have all the usual coffee options plus a fun fall menu. I didn’t catch the barista’s name, but she was super nice and quick with my order. I love checking out new coffee spots, and this one did not disappoint! The spiced apple latte was delicious, and both the chocolate croissant and blueberry muffin were really good too. There’s plenty of seating if you want to hang out or get some work done. Parking was a little confusing to figure out, and I think you have to pay for it after a certain time, but overall such a great spot!

This place is really cozy. The barista was friendly and answered all my questions. The coffee beans are light roast but they do have a dark roast pour over. Parking is in the garage down the road. They've got 3 different espresso roasts: regular, half caff, and decaf. We ordered a plain latte and a drip. Both were very good. One of the best lattes I've had. The vibe is so comfy in there. Exposed brick, taxidermy displays and couches. They also had some delicious looking pastries.

Chattahoochee Coffee is the perfect coffee shop to study or work remotely.  There is a very nice, spacious atmosphere with Wi-FI to work while enjoying some delicious coffee beverages.  There are a great variety of drinks, and the baristas are friendly and prompt.  I do wish there was a greater selection of pastries or snacks, as food choices are very limited.  Overall, it's a very relaxing, clean, warm, and welcoming coffee shop!

drink.name: Flat white
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review as one of the best ever, with mention of the Slayer espresso machine. Other drinks (chai latte, iced vanilla latte) were each mentioned positively only once, but the flat white received the most superlative praise.
drink.quote: One of the best flat whites I have ever had!

food.name: Chocolate chip cookie
food.reason: Only specific food item named positively in the reviews; another reviewer noted pastries sell out early, suggesting they're popular but no specific pastry was named.
food.quote: an iced vanilla latte and a chocolate chip cookie, both of which were delicious

reviewSample.text:
Nice place with good coffee. The drip was delicious and the espresso was well done. Baristas were friendly and seemed knowledgeable.

You only get 2 hours of internet access and then you’re cut off. I also notice how many baristas in the industry make assumptions about people’s coffee knowledge and just tell them things. I understand this kind of conversation as a cultural feature, and I also don’t prefer it. It becomes more of them telling me something that I already know and less a discussion about a mutually enjoyed nerdy topic.

They had a good selection of whole bean coffee. Nothing is labeled so I had to ask about prices, which I don’t like. It’s a bigger space in a brick building, with plenty of tables, couches, and chairs. You have to get lucky with parking, and the location is not accessible for many people. I had to walk two blocks, some parts of the sidewalk are cracked, and there’s at least one large step to enter the building. The floor is bare concrete inside with a step into another section with additional seating.

Stopped in today to grab a coffee and a pastry to work on so much needed letters.

Staff was super pleasant in the and the place itself is adorable. They were out of pastries (I got here at 12:30 on a Saturday) and they told me that they usually go pretty early in the morning - which I get.

I was recommended a chai latte and it was wonderful. A little pricey, but what is it in the city?

It’s a little tucked away but a great place to come if you need to get some reading or work done.

I’ll be back if I’m in the area.
One of the best flat whites I have ever had! Beautiful Slayer espresso machine. Cool loft space and on the beltline. Great vibe!

drink.name: Matcha Latte
drink.reason: Specifically praised as 'worth the drive' in a 5-star review; other lattes received mixed feedback about bitterness and weak flavors.
drink.quote: heard the matcha was great...totally worth the drive

food.name: Avocado Toast
food.reason: Mentioned positively in multiple reviews, the only food item with repeated specific praise.
food.quote: Avocado toast was good as well

reviewSample.text:
First time at this spot. The first thing you notice is the layout. Instead of the usual cramped tables and narrow walkways, this cafe offers a spacious, open floor plan.
Wide aisles comfortably accommodate wheelchairs and strollers. 10/10 for that.

We ordered dirty chai latte , caramel almond latte and avocado toast .. the coffee was delicious 😋, the barista clearly knew their craft and did not rush the order.
Avocado toast was good as well👍

Whether you’re stopping in for a quick caffeine fix, settling in for a work session, or looking for a space that genuinely welcomes individuals with special needs, this place delivers 💯
Great coffee, better mission, and a space that truly feels like it belongs to everyone.
Keep it up!

This cafe is a bit out of my way but I heard the matcha was great so I went to go see for myself. I ordered an iced sugar free vanilla matcha with oatmilk and it was totally work the drive! I came in close to closing so the only pastry available was the pumpkin muffin and it was so so yummy. I will definitely be returning, I'd love to try their food next time!

I stopped in for a lovely cappuccino and was so pleasantly surprised by this coffee shop! Loved the vibe, and it was a great combination of busy enough to feel fun, but not so crowded it was loud or you couldn't take your pick from several tables. Would love to visit again!

drink.name: Pistachio Honey Americano
drink.reason: Specifically named and praised as 'amazing' in a 5-star review highlighting specialty drinks. Other coffee mentions are generic or negative.
drink.quote: pistachio honey americano, which was amazing

food.name: Breakfast croissant sandwich
food.reason: The egg and cheese croissant received detailed praise for freshness and quality in a 5-star review, and breakfast sandwiches are a recurring highlight (Harrison breakfast sandwich also praised).
food.quote: real fresh eggs, buttery croissant, perfectly cooked

reviewSample.text: Was in town for work and needed a nice place to work quietly for work while enjoying a nice cup of coffee. This place was only a few minute walk and it was a cool little gem. This cozy little cafe was amazing. I got a pistachio honey americano, which was amazing. I also got the harrison breakfast sandwich was great. Music was not too loud you couldn’t work or take a zoom call. Staff was extremely friendly and helpful answering my million questions. Got to check this place out. Try one of their speciality drinks. The huge bonus is, when getting mixed coffee drinks they tend to be barely hot. This is the first place that I have not had to ask to make their coffee extra hot.

I loved the quirky interior! Very unique and chilled our cafe and that’s where the compliments end.

We ordered a Latte, Drip Coffee (dark roast) and a Croissant.

I am sorry to say but this place fell short of any expectations I had. The latte was flavourless, the croissant was flat, warm and oily. I’ve never had a croissant like that before so it’s a don’t waste your money moment for anyone reading this review.

The coffee was uninspiring.

The service was slow. I was given my latte and then the lady at the till started serving someone else! I had to ask her for the rest of my order.

Didn’t leave me eager to return which is a shame as I loved the interior.

Ps…one of your black leather chairs has a broken front leg. I’m glad I don’t weigh much otherwise I would have been on the floor.

Great hidden gem. Since it’s a small place, it was pretty hot, but that’s understandable. Delicious bagel sandwich and I saw a guy drawing another guy without him knowing! This is the type of local cafe you see in movies :).

drink.name: Matcha latte
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review for being not too sweet, with confirmation the matcha isn't pre-sweetened. Ube latte also mentioned positively.
drink.quote: My matcha was AMAZING! It was not too sweet

food.name: Sausage biscuit sandwich
food.reason: Strongly and specifically praised as homemade-tasting, huge, and worth the price. Other food mentions (cheese danish, pastries) were positive but less emphatic.
food.quote: tastes homemade and it is worth the price! It is huge and delicious

reviewSample.text:
Thus shop is doing all things right!

Let’s start with how we got here ! A friend sent me a picture of a Tik Tok picture featuring an ube latte! I responded with - when are we going? We decided we would wait until the weekend since we would have to drive from Cobb county to the city.

I started doing some research and did not see ube latte on the menu. I  called the phone number listed on yelp- but it appears it is just a cell number and went directly to voicemail. I went to the website and found an email address. I sent a basic question/ do you have an Ube latte on your menu? To my surprise I received a response! This simple response was exhibit A that they don’t play about customer service

On Saturday morning I arrived to the shop around 11am! The coffee shop is a neighborhood spot! As such, you will just have to find street parking in the neighborhood. I ended up parking a block away. This place was crowded!  I did have to wait in a line. However, I enjoyed checking out the item for sale while waiting in line. The book titles had me laughing ,  this is your go to spot for a unique gift!

We ended up ordering the following:
1. Hot Matcha latte with  ube and oat milk
2. Hot ube latte with oat milk
3. Cheese danish

NOTE: I did confirm that the matcha they use is NOT pre- sweetened. This is a huge plus in my book!!

The item came out quickly. All items weee served in to go containers. I am not sure if this is just standard practice even if you are dining in.

We were able to grab a table outside! However, this place was crowded and open seating was limited!

My matcha was AMAZING! It was not too sweet, which is how I like my matcha. The cheese danish was also delightful. My friend said she enjoyed her latte too. I will note that I did not taste the Ube flavor.

This  coffee shop is a vibe, but also  serves yo tasty drinks and treats! I would highly recommend checking this spot out !

I absolutely wish I had this place back at my hometown in Florida. You walk in and there is knick knacks everywhere but the employees immediately greet you with a warm welcome. Great selection of food with vegan options that is seperated from everything else and lots of coffee and tea options. The sausage biscuit sandwich tastes homemade and it is worth the price! It is huge and delicious. I had a latte with oat milk and some syrups and it was delicious as well! While waiting for your order there is a lot of eye catching decorations and books and more to buy and plenty of seating! I wish I could take this coffee shop back home with me it was that great and fantastic. I wish I got the employees names that worked there today but they were absolutely super nice and welcoming!

Ash is relatively new to Virginia Highlands but has most definitely taken the cake for the best coffee place in VaHi! The atmosphere is stunning with a small shop, lots of indoor tables, outdoor seating, and a couch nook in the back! They have nailed the decor! Ash is always full of people so depending on what time you go beware you may not find a table! They not only serve coffee but also delicious breakfast items and desserts! I would say 10/10 make sure to stop in here!!

drink.name: Adni Chai
drink.reason: The chai/Adni Chai is praised across multiple reviews for its rich cardamom aroma and homemade-style spicing.
drink.quote: the cardamom aroma is so rich, smooth, and full of flavor

food.name: Bee Bites
food.reason: Bee Bites received the most vivid, specific praise as a standout traditional Yemeni item; pastries are otherwise only generally mentioned.
food.quote: warmed and drizzled with golden, clear honey. Absolutely heavenly

reviewSample.text:
My first time visiting Atlanta, and I was thrilled to discover a Haraz Coffee House! I ordered their delicious and scrumptious Bee Bites — a traditional Yemeni bread filled with pockets of cream cheese, warmed and drizzled with golden, clear honey. Absolutely heavenly.

For my drink, I went with my all-time favorite: the Maleki. It’s a 500-year-old Yemeni recipe made with light-roast beans, coffee husks (the shell of the coffee bean after extraction), ginger, cinnamon, and milk to perfectly balance the flavors.

Everything was exceptional!

Haraz Coffee House – Atlanta is wonderfully spacious, with plenty of outdoor seating. One area features a vibrant green wall, elegant lighting, and outdoor heaters for the cooler weather — such a vibe.

Huge thanks to the owners Khuram and Rahib for the warm welcome and top-tier hospitality. You made my visit unforgettable!

I absolutely love this place! Upon my first visit I ordered a caramel macchiato with whole milk and my bf ordered a mocha latte. The drinks were sweet but not TOO sweet… just how I like it. The customer service was amazing. I visited a second time and my experience was even better. I ordered a salted caramel latte. I will def recommend this place to friends if they want a chill spot to study or grab a coffee on the go.

I’ve been wanting to try Haraz Coffee House ever since I drove by one day, and it did not disappoint. I’ve been on the hunt for a chai that tastes like my mom’s homemade chai, and this is the closest I’ve ever found…rich, perfectly spiced, and so comforting.

My husband ordered the Turkish coffee, which was excellent, and we sampled a few pastries as well…everything was absolutely delicious. The service was warm and attentive, which made the experience even better.

So glad we finally stopped in. We’ll definitely be back!

drink.name: Cappuccino
drink.reason: Mentioned positively in a 5-star review with specific praise for perfect foam and smooth espresso. The Chilly Goat and Matcha also got positive mentions but with less detail, and one cappuccino review was negative.
drink.quote: The foam was perfect, and the espresso had a rich, smooth flavor

food.name: Butter croissant
food.reason: The only specifically named food item in the reviews, and it received explicit love from a 4-star reviewer.
food.quote: Love the butter croissant

reviewSample.text: It was a great place from what google said but honestly the coffee's were not very good. Decorative but not good. I ordered a cappacino add mocha and caramel and i didnt taste anything but coffee. My friend ordered the matcha and said it just tasted like grass. I loved the decor though but probably won't try again when in ATL

My first experience at the Dancing goat was wonderful! Our barista Nate was so helpful and kind! He made the experience fun and light hearted. He made sure to Introduce himself and asked our names which I thought was a personal touch. My Matcha was so creamy! Overall great experience!

Stoped here specially for pour over. Love the concept and atmosphere.
Great service .
Chemic’s coffee was ok . Love the butter croissant 🥐.

drink.name: London Fog
drink.reason: Called one of the best in Atlanta in a 5-star review; other lattes (lavender chai, maple brown sugar) also praised but London Fog received the most superlative praise.
drink.quote: my London Fog was one of THEE best I've ever had in ATL

food.reason: Reviews only vaguely mention 'lite bites & breakfast pastries' with no specific food item named or praised.

reviewSample.text: Great spot near beltline that is serving great coffee & tea. I had the lavender Chai latte and it was really yummy. I'll be back

I absolutely loved my visit to Daily Dose Coffee! The shop was super clean and had such an inviting atmosphere—it’s the kind of place that makes you want to stay a while. The customer service was excellent; the staff was friendly, welcoming, and attentive. I ordered an iced maple brown sugar latte, and it was amazing—smooth, flavorful, and perfectly balanced. Definitely a spot I’ll be returning to. Highly recommend!

My friends and I really want to like this place, but it's very uncomfortable to go here. This coffee shop has pretty much become the apartment for the same 2-3 homeless men who are there open to close, every day. Taking up tables on the outside and inside, multiple large foul-smelling bags and luggage strewn about the front of the building, men going in and out of the bathroom a dozen times every 30 minutes, in and out the front door every 5 minutes, lurching over me and my friends and making strange comments, and things like that I find unappealing about returning.
I can understand being empathetic to the homeless, but coming here feels like I'm just intruding on their home at this point because it seems like just these 2-3 men are the only customer base this business is catering to, even they ever even buy anything. If you're going to house them there literally all day for months maybe consider helping them by hiring them, or at least doing something more for them than turning the business into what looks like a Greyhound station.

drink.name: Latte
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers specifically praise the latte (iced latte with almond milk called 'absolute perfection', and another calling it one of the best lattes in Atlanta with tastable flavor notes).
drink.quote: one of the best lattes I've had in Atlanta

food.name: Pineapple coffee cake
food.reason: Only specifically named food item with detailed praise; called 'a standout' with moist texture and balanced sweetness. The breakfast sandwich is also mentioned but with less specific praise.
food.quote: moist with small chunks of pineapple and just the right amount of streusel

reviewSample.text:
Recuerdos is a gem of a coffee shop! Tucked away in the beautifully renovated space beneath El Ponce, Recuerdos is a labor of love by husband and wife team Ivan and Carly. They source wonderful coffee from Mexico, which you can have in an espresso drink, as pourover, or incorporated into one of the creative seasonal beverages with syrups made in house. The café de olla holds a special place in my heart and is a love letter to Mexico. For non-coffee drinkers, they offer excellent matcha and chai lattes and seasonal offerings as well. Service is warm and attentive, it's clear that there's love and pride and attention to detail in everything about Recuerdos. If you have your drink on site, it will be served in beautiful ceramic mug designed by local artist Charlotte Smith! Last but not least, there's a small selection of pastries and a fantastic breakfast sandwich (add avocado!) from Chef Claudia of Bar Ana, with whom Recuerdos shares the space.

I love Recuerdos and so will you.

Logistics: you can park for free in the El Ponce parking lot. Enter from the back, through the green door on the right that says Bar Ana. There's free WiFi and outlets, but honestly, I wish folks wouldn't use the space as their office

Gorgeous, cozy, memorable speakeasy with such strong coffee. Every detail in the venue was planned purposefully, making for an authentic and well-rounded experience. Seating is incredibly limited given the ever-long line out-the-door, but the bar seating we conveniently got was so comfortable because of the cushioned high stools.

The Chai Latte is very strong and easily keeps you up the whole day. Truly a great drink. Overall, would recommend finding and visiting!

The “secret” coffee spot inside Bar ANA is called REGUERDOS, and wow — what a cup of coffee.

I drink a lot of coffee, and this might be one of the best lattes I’ve had in Atlanta. You can actually taste the flavor notes — smooth, rich, and beautifully presented. It honestly took me back to some of my favorite coffee shops in Kenya, where the flavor really speaks for itself.

Customer service was just as impressive. Cheers to Sasha (I believe that was her name) for the warm welcome, and to our barista — whose name I didn’t catch — for crafting such a delicious latte.

Highly recommend this spot, whether you’re grabbing a quick coffee or meeting someone to connect. In Sweden, we have a tradition called fika — slowing down to enjoy coffee and conversation. This would be the perfect place for that.

Opening hours: Thursday–Monday, 7AM–3PM
“MEXICAN ROOTED, ATLANTA RAISED”

Cheers for an amazing cup — see you tomorrow. ☕✨

Cheers,

BN

drink.name: Banana Pudding Matcha
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers highlight the matcha drinks, with the banana pudding matcha specifically praised in a 5-star review. One reviewer says matcha is what they order 90% of the time. A 2-star review criticized it, but positive specific mentions outweigh.
drink.quote: The banana pudding matcha, ube latte... all incredible

food.name: Rainbow Bagel
food.reason: The rainbow bagel is specifically called out in a glowing 5-star review, with a memorable moment of it being 'perfectly buttered.' It's the most vividly praised food item named in the reviews.
food.quote: perfectly buttered rainbow bagel

reviewSample.text: So excited the new location is open after so many months. Well worth the wait! I’m a huge coffee drinker but their matcha is so good that that’s what I get 90% of the time. Mad’s matcha is my favorite. The Spicy Nini is also really good. Love the welcoming atmosphere and all the creative drink options. I really appreciate all the work that goes into their specials too. Definitely one of my favorite spots in Atlanta!

I spent two weeks in Atlanta for work. Every single morning started at Schāf Shop. By the end, I didn't want to leave.

Lindzey and Taylor have created something I've never experienced anywhere else: a coffee-and-skate shop where the hospitality is so genuine it borders on unreasonable. The banana pudding matcha, ube latte, the ginger turmeric tonic, the rainbow bagels, mom's tarts...all incredible. But it's the amazing humans behind the counter who make this place magic.

On my last morning, Three Little Birds came on the hi-fi just as Lindzey brought me my perfectly buttered rainbow bagel. I almost lost it. That's Schāf Shop. A place where the moments that matter find you.

Go. Become a regular. You'll understand.

Loved the vibe immediately! Comfortable seating, skateboard shop, vibes were perfect. I have a sweet tooth & the Carmel latte wasn’t quite sweet enough - the owner was really sweet & personally made another one for me, instead of just adding more Carmel.. ended up being that they use unsweetened almond milk- but the second one was perfect!
I will be back!

drink.name: Iced Strawberry Matcha
drink.reason: Enthusiastically praised in a 5-star review as the best matcha ever, with specific detail about strawberry puree and cold foam. Other drink mentions (chai latte, hazelnut latte) were positive but less standout.
drink.quote: the best matcha I have ever had

food.name: Warmed croissant
food.reason: Food items received limited praise overall. The croissant was mentioned positively in a 5-star review, while the Fox-Shaped Cookie was called disappointing and the bagel sandwich had service issues.
food.quote: I also got a warmed croissant

reviewSample.text: Came here to work for a bit and liked it! I parked in the garage and they validate for 2 hours. It’s cute and cozy inside lots of seating, tables, and outlets. Staff was quick with drinks and the chai latte was good. I also got a warmed croissant. Overall I would definitely come back. There’s outdoor seating as well for a nice day.

I was looking for some new matcha I hadn't tried yet in Buckhead and I decided to try Foxtail Coffee out! I had to park in the deck ($10 for 2 hours) which I didn't like, but it was honestly worth it. I asked for an iced strawberry matcha, and he asked me if I wanted the cold foam or just strawberry puree- I said both. It costed about $9.66 with tip, which although expensive, it is unfortunately reasonable compared to nearby coffee shops. It was BEAUTIFUL and also the best matcha I have ever had!!! I drank the whole thing in maybe 5 minutes. However, it was very sweet (which I love) and I definitely tasted the strawberry flavor more than the matcha flavor. I personally LOVED it but if you aren't a huge fan of sweet drinks it probably won't be for you. The atmosphere is super cute and the staff was nice. If you are looking for a sweet strawberry matcha I DEFINITELY recommend!

Coffee was very strong, which was a very welcome detail! Interior and exterior are both very aesthetic, and the place is perfect for a quiet work spot or catch-up with friends. Service was also phenomenal - they were so nice and helpful with parking.

Sadly, while the coffee itself was great, the Fox-Shaped Cookie ended up being a disappointing shortbread. Nonetheless, I would love to return here one day to get some work done!

drink.name: Pour Over
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers highlight the pour over as exceptional, with one calling it among the best coffee in Atlanta and ideal for sitting and enjoying.
drink.quote: one of the best cups of coffee you can get in Atlanta

food.name: Spicy Sweet Potato Empanada
food.reason: Specifically praised for great flavor and balanced spice in a 5-star review; the only food item with detailed positive feedback.
food.quote: spicy sweet potato empanada had great flavor without being too spicy

reviewSample.text:
At the edge of Midtown lies East Pole, an endearing staple of Poncey-Highland. I love that this place is constantly filled with people who are either working away at their computers, coming and going as part of their busy mornings, or chatting with friends and family while their pets sit nearby. Essentially, this is a lovely spot to get acquainted with the local neighborhood, from what I could see.

The demand for drinks is extensive, owing to the care they put into their deliverables. Pairing the beachy interior decor with the spacious nature of the venue, though, the wait is pleasant, as one can watch the barista masterfully put together a line of orders at the counter.

Drinks are very expensive, so I would recommend trying this place out for a decadent morning coffee. For one, I found the seasonal drink to be rich and as autumn-themed as they said it would be. Plus, once you grab your coffee, the surrounding area is beautiful to walk around. A great start to the day!

Great coffee shop. I tried the pour over and had a coffee from Ethiopia. The bagel I had with cream cheese was also really good. I enjoyed the inside space and really appreciated the two hours of free parking. This is a great spot to come work or have a meeting for one to two hours on weekdays. On weekends I think it’s limited to one hour.

There's so many things about this place that I want to love. I think as a study cafe, it works if you get here early enough when it's not packed or peak hours. This East Pole location doesn't have too much seating. There's a few tables in the back, coffee counter seating, and high table chairs. There was a couple of outlets upside down by the high chairs at the windows. I came on a warm, sunny day so it was nice to sit at the pink metal tables outside. The cortado had a beautiful presentation and just enough of a caffeine hit. The guy at the counter was super nice when I asked him for recommendations and very conversational. Even when it was busy, I felt welcomed and was intrigued by his questions.

drink.name: Flash Brew
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review as not bitter and easy to drink; other reviews consistently praise the coffee overall but Flash Brew is the only named standout drink.
drink.quote: The Flash Brew is great if you're not wanting something sweet

food.name: Breakfast Crunchwrap with bacon
food.reason: Received the most enthusiastic, specific food praise in a 5-star review; pastries were only described as 'good' by comparison.
food.quote: breakfast Crunchwrap with bacon was out of this world

reviewSample.text: Very crowded in this location but the food, coffee, and service were great! The Flash Brew is great if you’re not wanting something sweet. It’s not super bitter and is easy to drink. The speciality lattes are all great and on the sweeter side. Definitely would recommend stopping by for a quick breakfast. The area is also very nice and walkable. You will have to find street parking.

Definitely coming back here. Love this spot! Great service, & the drinks were delicious!! The breakfast Crunchwrap with bacon was out of this world. Absolutely mouth watering. Hot, crisp, & fresh. Had plenty of seating, inside, & an outdoor patio as well. The staff were friendly as well.

I’ve actually never been here but if their everything is as good as their sourcing and roasting, this is a top notch spot!
I ordered their beans through Bean Box (a great out of town coffee lovers subscription). Their packaging is catchy, playful, and fun and the roasts match these vibes.
I cracked open my first bag of the Ethiopian Funky Wush Wush this morning and got the rig all setup. It shined once the coffee temp hit 115-120F showing its youthful, new, and nostalgic profile. After my first cup, I already can’t wait for the afternoon pour!
If we’re ever in ATL, Perc first, everything else later. Thanks for the morning smiles Perc team!

drink.name: Mocha
drink.reason: A 5-star reviewer specifically praised it as a 'real mocha' rather than the syrupy versions common elsewhere. The pumpkin spice latte also got positive mention, but the mocha received the most enthusiastic specific praise.
drink.quote: Had a fantastic mocha...A real mocha, not one of those syrupy things

food.name: Pastries
food.reason: The only food item with positive specific mention, praised in a 5-star review for tasting like home. Other specific food mentions (cinnamon roll) were negative.
food.quote: pastries that taste a little like home

reviewSample.text: Stopped by to finish up some work on my laptop after an early lunch. Great remote work environment, plenty of seating, and PASTRIES that taste a little like home. The pumpkin spice latte was pretty tasty too. Not too sweet, not too milky. It was different from other PSL I’ve had this season. Great coffee shop overall! Oh and I almost forgot you can miss the building if you don’t pay attention. Lol.

A Wonderful Cafe Experience with Room for Improvement

This cafe truly captivated us with its engaging atmosphere, friendly staff, and interesting food menu. The range of options was impressive and the quality of the dishes was equally commendable. Our orders were executed with expertise and the balance of flavors showcased the culinary prowess of the kitchen team.

One of the standout experiences was trying their juice selections, which were nothing short of remarkable. Expertly crafted and bursting with natural flavors, they were a joy to sip on. Each mouthful was a refreshing experience that made us appreciate the careful thought and precision put into creating these concoctions. A big, well-deserved BRAVO to the team responsible!

Despite the food and service being top-notch, cleanliness in certain areas left much to be desired. While the cafe interior, including the kitchen area, was spotless, the state of the restroom was a letdown. We found it to be quite unclean, which was surprising considering the otherwise immaculate state of the establishment. This is a crucial area where the cafe needs immediate attention, as it is a fundamental aspect of any dining experience and can significantly influence a customer's perception of the business.

The staff was a definite highlight of our visit. From the moment we stepped in, we were greeted by warm smiles and friendly chatter. Their professionalism and genuine friendliness added a personal touch to our experience. Their dedication to maintaining a clean environment was evident in the dining and kitchen areas, and we hope that the same level of attention is extended to the restroom in the future.

For those interested, I captured a snapshot of the extensive food menu and posted it . You'll find that their offerings cater to a variety of tastes and dietary preferences.

To conclude, we truly enjoyed our time at this cafe and the overall experience was delightful. The food, drinks, and staff more than compensated for the one area of concern. However, I trust that the management will address the restroom issue promptly. Once this is taken care of, the cafe will undeniably be a five-star experience.

Had a fantastic mocha at Press and Grind last week.  A real mocha...not one of those syrupy things everyone is making these days.  And the staff were warm and welcoming as well as efficient.  Met a friend for a coffee and we had a lovely time there.  We will be back.

drink.name: Latte
drink.reason: Lattes are the most frequently praised drink, described as 'really delicious' and 'masterfully created,' with a specific Caramel Vanilla Half Caf Latte called 'excellent.'
drink.quote: masterfully created latte

food.name: Chocolate croissant
food.reason: The only specifically named food item, described positively in a 5-star review.
food.quote: The chocolate croissant they had available was really good

reviewSample.text: This is 5 star without a question in my mind. Was in Atlanta for a few days in Jan and came across this cute coffee shop. Highly recommend. There was only one worker but his service was outstanding! He was multitasking and communicating well with customers while he worked so fast but didn’t miss a beat. Coffee hit the spot! Do check it out if you get the chance!

Clean, bright coffee shop with plenty of seating. Came here to kill a little time and enjoy a latte and some cold brew - both were really delicious. The chocolate croissant they had available was really good, too.

I have to give a shout-out to this place for two reasons in particular:

Prices are fair! Since COVID, I can't help but feel like some shops have taken advantage of the situation with inflation to really gouge their customers. Not so with Kula!

Second: they don't default to disposable cups if you're having your drink at the shop. Soooo many places have taken up this practice over the years, and it's honestly really disappointing that so much plastic and other waste isn't a concern to them. The fact that you can enjoy your drink in a real cup or glass just enhances the experience for me, and I think Kula deserves to be commended for it.

Great job to everyone at the shop! I look forward to coming back, and I highly recommend it to anyone searching for some coffee in the Buckhead. Don't even consider that overpriced garbage at Starbucks - come here for your coffee instead!

Excellent hot Caramel Vanilla Half Caf Latte!  Peaceful coffee shop with relaxing decor, and friendly atmosphere.  Reasonable pricing  here too!  Tried this place instead of the other place across the street, and so glad I did.  This was exactly the ticket.
